audit_items.proof_trace: The only dictDelete call on dict entries is in latencyResetEvent, which uses dictGetSafeIterator; dictNext explicitly saves iter->nextEntry = iter->entry->next before returning each entry (dict.c:590), so the caller may safely delete the returned entry. After dictDelete(server.latency_events, event) at L145, the freed pointer `event` is not accessed again in the loop body. All sds/sparkline allocations are freed exactly once at their single use site.
